places.name: Maungawhau (Mount Eden)
places.description: The platform atop this verdant volcano was built with the help of a royal elephant.

places.name: Sky Tower
places.description: A New Zealand communications tower stands as the tallest structure in the Southern Hemisphere.

places.name: Browns Island
places.description: This quiet island in the middle of a bustling city is actually a volcano dotted with Māori ruins.

places.name: Abandoned WWII Structures of Motutapu Island
places.description: A network of fortifications that never saw conflict.

places.name: Symonds Street Cemetery
places.description: One of Auckland's oldest cemeteries was partially demolished during the construction of a major highway in the 1960s.

places.name: Stony Batter Historic Reserve
places.description: Large World War II gun emplacements and tunnel systems remain hidden near a remote end of Waiheke Island.

places.name: Waikumete Cemetery
places.description: Home to the unidentified remains of the 1979 Mt. Erebus Disaster.

places.name: OnDo Giant Noodle Sculpture
places.description: In an Auckland neighborhood, looming chopsticks lift noodles from the sidewalk.
